symptoms.character: sharp, constant, right upper quadrant under the ribs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: RUQ pain is one limb of Charcot's triad.
Also produces RUQ pain; distinguished by the presence of obstructive jaundice.
Rigors indicate bacteraemia; with biliary obstruction this is the sepsis limb of Charcot's triad.
Intense rigors are atypical for viral/autoimmune hepatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Obstructive jaundice (scleral icterus, yellow skin) with dark urine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaundice from common-bile-duct obstruction is the cholestasis limb of the triad; dark urine reflects conjugated hyperbilirubinuria.
Frank jaundice points beyond isolated gallbladder inflammation to ductal obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe nausea with bilious vomiting, unable to tolerate oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Common in biliary sepsis; also precludes oral therapy and worsens dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Alert, no confusion or fainting at present (pertinent negative for Reynolds' pentad)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute ascending cholangitis with biliary sepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of confusion/hypotension means Reynolds' pentad is not yet complete, but does not downgrade the emergency — deterioration is anticipated and must be safety-netted.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute ascending cholangitis with biliary sepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective sepsis physiology — clinician-measured; the AI Doctor infers systemic upset from rigors, toxicity and inability to hydrate, not from a measured BP/HR.

symptoms.symptom_name: RUQ tenderness with guarding and a palpable, tender liver edge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localises the pathology to the biliary tree/liver; an in-person examination finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain — the T3+ urgent in-person pathway exists to compensate for exactly this.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dilated common bile duct with obstructing stone (imaging)

investigation_details.expected_result: Dilated common bile duct, likely choledocholithiasis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Obstruction and its level are not localised, delaying decompression.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biliary dilatation on imaging is a diagnostic criterion (TG18) and guides decompression — a hospital step, unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Septic bloods (raised WCC/CRP, deranged LFTs, raised bilirubin, positive blood cultures)

investigation_details.investigation_name: FBC, CRP, LFTs, bilirubin, lactate, blood cultures
investigation_details.expected_result: Inflammatory response with cholestatic LFT pattern and bacteraemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity grading and antibiotic targeting are not possible; sepsis may be under-recognised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute ascending cholangitis with biliary sepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic-inflammation and cholestasis criteria (TG18); venous bloods cannot be obtained in telehealth.
Pre-existing gallstones are the substrate for an obstructing common-bile-duct stone driving cholangitis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The co-occurrence of RUQ pain, fever with rigors and jaundice is the classic triad of ascending cholangitis and, on a gallstone background, is a biliary-sepsis emergency until proven otherwise.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment with FBC, CRP, LFTs, bilirubin, lactate and blood cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades biliary sepsis; must occur in hospital, not via telehealth.
Confirms biliary dilatation/obstruction and guides ERCP; a hospital investigation.
Definitive source control for the obstructing stone; the AI Doctor recognises the need and escalates, but cannot perform or order it directly.
Cholangitis with sepsis requires IV antibiotics and decompression in hospital; a remote oral-antibiotic script would be an unsafe substitute for emergency escalation.
